notes: Hand-curated public facts from the first HumoyunGPT corpus.

chunks.text: Khumoyun Nasipkulov is a Mathematics and Economics undergraduate student at the Hong Kong University of Science and Technology (HKUST). His portfolio describes him as passionate about education and mentorship.
Education: HKUST, BSc Mathematics and Economics, Year 3, September 2023 to present, Hong Kong. Global Learners scholarship recipient, full tuition fees for four years.

Education also includes Vrije Universiteit Amsterdam for a spring semester exchange beginning January 2026, Vin University in Hanoi for a fully funded summer exchange in Global Political Economy in 2024, and Westminster International University in Tashkent for a Certificate of International Foundation Studies with a full tuition academic scholarship.
Work experience: Database Developer at HKUST Business School, External and Alumni Relations office, managing Excel databases, data processing automation, alumni records, and administrative records.

Work experience: Walt Disney intern in Florida, USA, June 2025 to August 2025, guest service in a fast-paced multicultural environment, international teamwork, problem solving under pressure, and professional development in leadership and communication.

Work experience: Teaching Assistant and Grader at Art of Problem Solving Academy, remote from California, December 2024 to present. He assists live math olympiad classes for high school students preparing for AMC, AIME, USAMO, IMC, and IMO, and grades assignments for more than 100 students weekly.

Work experience: Intern at the Department of Small and Medium Businesses, Ministry of Finance and Economics of the Republic of Uzbekistan, June 2024 to August 2024. He researched MSME sector development, international best practices, reports, case studies, and policy recommendations.

Work experience: Mathematics Teacher at Alpha Academy in Sirdaryo, Uzbekistan, May 2023 to August 2023. He was the first SAT teacher in his region, teaching SAT math, sequences, trigonometry, and combinatorics.
Leadership: President of HKUST Mathematics and Economics Club from March 2024 to present, leading a club with more than 100 students and seven executive committee secretaries.

Leadership: Founder and Head Mentor of NASEEB EDU from September 2023 to present. The program prepares students for admission to top universities, has guided more than 100 students into top 100 universities worldwide, provides personalized support, and communicates regularly with parents.

Leadership: Event Manager at Ibrat Farzandlari under the Youth Affairs Agency of the Republic of Uzbekistan from May 2022 to present. He organizes summer camps, logistics, mentorship programs, and invites students from prestigious universities as speakers and mentors.

Awards and recognition include El-Yurt Umidi Foundation scholarship holder in June 2023, winner of the Mirzo Ulugbek Vorislari startup competition in May 2023, gold medalist in MAA AMC 10/12 in November 2021, bronze medalist in Southeast Asian Mathematical Olympiad in September 2020, and first place in the Republican Mathematical and Physics Olympiad in December 2019.

Skills: Python, SQL, R, HTML/CSS, Microsoft Office. Languages: Uzbek native, English fluent, Russian conversational, Chinese basic. Public contact from the portfolio: knasipkulov@connect.ust.hk, LinkedIn khumoyun-nasipkulov, Telegram @humoyun_com.

Telegram channel bio for humoyun.com says: Founder @naseeb_edu, Mathematics and Economics at HKUST, Intern at Disney, TA at AoPS, EY Academy, Intern at MEF, EYUF, Database Developer at HKUST Business School, Ambassador at Association of Exporters of Uzbekistan.

The public Telegram channel humoyun.com is categorized under Uzbekistan and Education, with around 665 subscribers and public posts about study abroad, scholarships, mathematics, faith, family, travel, Hong Kong, and student mentorship.

Humoyun recommended Stipendium Hungaricum as a scholarship plan B and listed full tuition fee exemption, monthly stipend, accommodation contribution, medical insurance, and visa fee coverage. He framed Schengen visa access as an important opportunity.
Humoyun posted that he became a Student Ambassador, selected among only 100 out of 1100 undergraduate students, roughly a 9 percent acceptance rate.
A recurring belief from his channel: be careful with intentions, because intentions have a property of becoming real.
A recurring motivational line from the channel: do not allow the future to shape you, shape it. We are the future.
Humoyun wrote that one meaningful connection can open more doors than fifty job applications.

Humoyun announced SpeakUp, a human-AI conversation bridge for language learning, aimed at people who know grammar but freeze when speaking. Features included practicing speaking with AI coach Alex, IELTS preparation, no judgment, no scheduling, and no hourly tutor fee.
NASEEB EDU post: students received full-ride scholarships from HKUST. Humoyun often closes education posts with the phrase 'Sizga ham naseeb qilsin', meaning may it be destined for you too.

Ramadan Eid post: he encouraged students dreaming of studying abroad not to stop trying, to dream and make dua, to spend the holiday with family, and to receive parents' prayers. The voice is faith-aware, warm, and community-minded.

Humoyun reflected on a podcast that changed how he thinks about choosing a team, improving home environment, setting goals, and what to do when goals go the opposite way. He recommends content that shapes life direction, not just entertainment.

Humoyun wrote about Schengen as both a historical agreement and a personal symbol. Winning Stipendium Hungaricum in June 2023 made Europe feel reachable to a student who had not even traveled to a neighboring country before age 19.

Humoyun asked why Uzbekistan has fewer engineers and argued that dreams alone are not enough. Parents should expose children to engineering, museums, machines, and how things work from early childhood. He noticed fathers bringing children to the BMW museum in Munich and wants to be that kind of father.

China Daily Asia video description says Khumoyun Nasipkulov is an Uzbek finance student in Hong Kong. He was drawn by Hong Kong's role as an international financial hub, generous scholarships, career options, and opportunities to understand the Greater Bay Area and the Chinese mainland. He said the four years in Hong Kong have been the best years of his life and hopes to serve as a bridge linking Uzbekistan and Hong Kong after graduation.

El-Yurt Umidi Foundation post mentions Humoyun Nasipqulov as studying Mathematical Economics at Hong Kong University of Science and Technology and appearing in a Kun.uz conversation with other scholarship recipients.

Bloomberg for Education certificate page says Khumoyun Nasipkulov completed Bloomberg Finance Fundamentals on February 28, 2026. The course covers money flows through the economy, investment strategies, responsible investing, and finance career paths.
